page: Amazon Web Services r5dn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor with 16 dedicated virtual CPUs (8 physical cores with hyper-threading) operating at 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. Storage is supported by a 600 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 16 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates balanced performance across memory and compute operations. The instance achieves average multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 13544.88 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 7031.0. Single-core performance is relatively weaker, as shown by a stress-ng single-core score of 1285.48 and a weak single-threaded gzip compression rate of 21.9 GB/sec. However, memory bandwidth remains solid, reaching an average read-only large block speed of 88.79 GB/sec and a write speed of 47.79 GB/sec. Database operations are supported by a Redis SET throughput of 13.59 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5dn.4xlarge offers a high-density memory configuration paired with fast local NVMe storage, reducing the need for external network-attached storage for high-throughput database operations. The primary tradeoff is the moderate single-core processing speed, which makes it less suitable for compute-bound single-threaded tasks. This instance is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, database hosting, caching layers, and applications requiring high network and EBS throughput alongside local scratch space.
description: Amazon Web Services r5dn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance designed for high-throughput workloads. Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs (8 physical cores) running at 3.1 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The hardware configuration includes a 600 GB local NVMe SSD and a 16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show average multi-core CPU and memory performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 13544.88 and a Redis SET throughput of 13.59 million operations per second, though single-core performance is relatively weak. This instance offers a cost-effective balance of high memory capacity and fast local storage, making it well-suited for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, caching layers, and workloads requiring high network and EBS optimization.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5dn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, a 600 GB local NVMe SSD, and 16 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5dn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 600 GB NVMe SSD for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed DDR4 memory, and local NVMe SSD storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 3.1 GHz clock speed
128 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8 GB of RAM per vCPU
600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-speed scratch space
16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS and data transfer
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and data processing
